description: Two threads read-modify-write the same state without synchronization, losing updates and triggering undefined behavior.
body: Failure mode: shared mutable state (a counter, map, or struct) is read and written from multiple threads with no lock, atomic, or owner, so updates are lost (read-modify-write interleaves) and in many languages the data race is undefined behavior that may corrupt unrelated memory. Detection signals: a race detector flags conflicting accesses; a counter ends lower than the number of increments; intermittent corruption under load. Do not assume a single statement is atomic or that it 'works in practice'. Safer fallback: route every access through one mutex, atomic, or owning task, then prove it with a race-detector clean run and a stress test that reconciles the final state.
